title: Zara and the Phrasal Verb Swarm
description: Phrasal verbs — the bane of every learner. Zara shows that LOOK UP ≠ LOOK AFTER ≠ LOOK FOR, and none of them has much to do with looking at the ceiling.
summary_pt_br: Phrasal verbs essenciais: LOOK UP/AFTER/FOR, GET UP/OVER/ALONG, PUT ON/OFF/UP WITH, BREAK DOWN/UP. A chave: não traduza literalmente — decore como blocos. Regra de separável x inseparável.

scenes.subtitle_en: One verb, a preposition, and a whole new meaning
scenes.subtitle_pt: Um verbo, uma preposição, um sentido novo

scenes.text_en:
Zara wrote three phrases on the whiteboard:
    LOOK UP a word in the dictionary.
    LOOK AFTER a child.
    LOOK FOR your keys.
'Same verb,' she said, 'three completely different meanings. Welcome to phrasal verbs.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Zara escreveu três frases no quadro:
    LOOK UP — procurar uma palavra no dicionário.
    LOOK AFTER — cuidar de uma criança.
    LOOK FOR — buscar as chaves.
'Mesmo verbo,' ela disse. 'Três sentidos completamente diferentes. Bem-vindos aos phrasal verbs.'

scenes.title: Phrasal verbs — don't translate, memorize
scenes.body_en: A phrasal verb is a verb + preposition/particle that together mean something NEW. You can't work out 'look after' from 'look' + 'after'. Two rules: (1) SEPARABLE verbs let the object go between — 'pick UP the kids' OR 'pick the kids UP'. With pronouns, you MUST separate: 'pick them UP'. (2) INSEPARABLE verbs keep the particle close: 'look AFTER the kids' — never 'look the kids after'.
scenes.body_pt: Phrasal verb = verbo + partícula = sentido NOVO. Não tente deduzir. Duas regras: SEPARÁVEL aceita o objeto no meio ('pick up the kids' / 'pick the kids up'). Com pronome, TEM que separar: 'pick them up'. INSEPARÁVEL não separa nunca: 'look after them'.

examples.en: Pick UP the kids / Pick the kids UP / Pick THEM UP
examples.pt: Separável: tudo certo; com pronome TEM que separar.

examples.en: Look after them. NOT 'look them after'.

scenes.title: Ten phrasal verbs for the week

exercise.question: Complete: 'Could you ___ ___ the kids tonight? I have an interview.' (cuidar de)
exercise.explanation: LOOK AFTER = to take care of. Inseparable — the object goes AFTER the phrasal verb.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Pick up them at the airport
Pick them up at the airport
Pick at the airport them up
Pick them at the airport up
exercise.explanation: Separable verb + pronoun → you MUST separate: 'pick them up'.

exercise.question: 'My car ___ on the way to the airport.' (quebrou)
exercise.explanation: BROKE DOWN = stopped working (car, machine). BROKE UP = ended a relationship. Very different!
exercise.hint_pt_br: Broke DOWN = parou de funcionar. Broke UP = terminar relacionamento. Não confunda.

scenes.location_pt: Sala dos professores, Zara corrigindo provas

turns.en: I get along with most of the students.
turns.pt: Me dou bem com a maioria dos alunos.

turns.en: And Joca? Do you put up with him?
turns.pt: E o Joca? Você tolera ele?

turns.en: I put up with him. Barely.

exercise.explanation: Five essential phrasal verbs. Learn them in full sentences — 'I put up with my noisy neighbors'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco essenciais. Decore em frases completas, não isolados.

exercise.question: Complete: 'The meeting was ___ ___ until next week.' (adiado)
exercise.explanation: PUT OFF = postpone. 'The meeting was put off' (passive).
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'Put off' = adiar. 'Put off until next week' é frase típica.
